diagrams.title: Binary Trees with 3 Nodes
diagrams.description: All 5 distinct binary tree structures with 3 nodes, corresponding to C₃ = 5
elements: Tree 1: Root with left child having left grandchild (left-left chain)
Tree 2: Root with left child having right grandchild (left-right chain)
Tree 3: Root with left and right children each being leaves
Tree 4: Root with right child having left grandchild (right-left chain)
Tree 5: Root with right child having right grandchild (right-right chain)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the enumeration principle: for n=3, left subtree can have 0,1,2 nodes giving C₀C₂ + C₁C₁ + C₂C₀ = 1×2 + 1×1 + 2×1 = 5 trees.

diagrams.title: Dyck Path to Binary Tree Mapping
diagrams.description: Bijection between Dyck paths of length 2n and binary trees with n+1 nodes
elements: Up-step U=(1,+1): Add left child and move to it
Start at root, sequence of U and D traces tree
Example: UUDDUDD corresponds to tree with root having left child with left leaf
diagrams.purpose: Show combinatorial equivalence: Catalan numbers count both binary trees and Dyck paths, establishing the fundamental bijection that proves the enumeration formula.

diagrams.title: Full vs Complete vs Perfect Binary Trees
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of tree types with 7 nodes showing relationships
elements: Perfect (full + complete): All levels filled, 7 nodes, height 2
Complete: Levels 0,1 full, level 2 filled left-to-right
Full: Every node has 0 or 2 children, but level 2 not fully filled
Draw each type to show containment: Perfect ⊂ Full, Perfect ⊂ Complete, but Full ⊄ Complete
diagrams.purpose: Clarify definitions and relationships: Perfect ⇒ Full ⇒ Complete, but converse does not hold. Important for tree counting applications.
